titleEn: Brazil poultry sector eyes steady growth in 2026
titleZh: 巴西家禽业展望2026年稳步增长
summaryZh: 据Cepea预测，巴西家禽市场2026年将继续扩张，出口和消费均有望增长，前提是避免禽流感新疫情。人均鸡肉消费预计达47.3公斤，产量将增至1473万吨，出口增长2.4%。巴西占全球鸡肉出口约三分之一，尽管面临禽流感风险，但凭借高生物安全水平和技术能力，行业仍保持领先地位。
featuredReason: 该新闻反映了巴西家禽业在禽流感风险下的增长预期，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解全球禽业市场动态和生物安全需求具有参考价值。
contentFull: Markets & policy Market trends Brazil poultry sector eyes steady growth in 2026 Exports, consumption seen rising if bird flu avoided 26 January 2026 1 minute read By: Global Ag Media South America Brazil’s poultry market is expected to continue expanding in 2026, supported by higher exports, supply aligned with domestic and international demand, and favourable producer margins, according to Cepea.The outlook, however, depends on the absence of new cases of avian influenza, particularly during peak migratory bird movements.The Brazilian Animal Protein Association estimates per capita chicken consumption at 47.3 kg in 2026, up 1.2% from the previous year.Brazil accounts for about one-third of global chicken meat exports and has maintained its leading position despite isolated trade restrictions linked to bird flu. Cepea researchers estimate exports could rise 2.4% in 2026, while chicken meat production is forecast to increase 3.8% from a year earlier to 14.73 million tonnes.Achieving this growth will require strict sanitary controls, as outbreaks at commercial farms can trigger immediate trade bans by importing countries, as occurred in May 2025. Cepea points to the need for ongoing monitoring of the H5N1 virus, amid recent outbreaks in Europe, the United States and Japan associated with migratory bird flows.Despite these risks, Brazil’s poultry sector benefits from a high level of biosecurity and strong technical and commercial capacity to respond to potential cases, a capability demonstrated during 2025. translationZh:
据Cepea预测，巴西家禽市场预计将在2026年继续扩张，这得益于出口增长、供应与国内外需求相匹配以及有利的生产者利润率。

然而，这一前景取决于没有新的禽流感病例，尤其是在候鸟迁徙高峰期。

巴西动物蛋白协会估计，2026年人均鸡肉消费量为47.3公斤，比上一年增长1.2%。

巴西约占全球鸡肉出口的三分之一，尽管因禽流感而出现零星的贸易限制，但仍保持领先地位。Cepea研究人员估计，2026年出口可能增长2.4%，而鸡肉产量预计将比上年增长3.8%，达到1473万吨。

要实现这一增长，必须实施严格的卫生控制，因为商业农场的疫情爆发可能立即引发进口国的贸易禁令，就像2025年5月发生的那样。Cepea指出，鉴于欧洲、美国和日本近期出现与候鸟迁徙相关的疫情，需要持续监测H5N1病毒。

尽管存在这些风险，巴西家禽业受益于高水平的生物安全以及应对潜在病例的强大技术和商业能力，这一能力在2025年已得到证明。
contentHtml:
Brazil poultry sector eyes steady growth in 2026 
Exports, consumption seen rising if bird flu avoided
